title: Inuit Nunangat: Our Land, Our Future — ITK Strategic Plan
description: Inuit Tapiriit Kanatami's strategic vision for Inuit Nunangat, documenting Inuit priorities for land-based knowledge, cultural continuity, and community-driven education. ITK represents all four Inuit regions of Canada.
communityEndorsement: Published directly by ITK, the national representational organization for Inuit in Canada. Represents all four Inuit regions: Nunatsiavut, Nunavik, Nunavut, and Inuvialuit Settlement Region.

title: Uqaujjuusiat: Words of Wisdom from the Past
description: Collection of Inuit elder wisdom and traditional knowledge compiled by Inhabit Media, the Inuit-owned publishing house based in Iqaluit, Nunavut. Includes practical knowledge about Arctic living and seasonal practices.
communityEndorsement: Published by Inhabit Media, an Inuit-owned publisher founded in Iqaluit, Nunavut, dedicated to preserving and sharing Inuit knowledge through Inuit-controlled publication.

title: Nanuit Sinnaktangit: Inuit Knowledge and Arctic Ecology
description: Community-based participatory research documenting Inuit ecological knowledge about sea ice travel, Arctic wildlife, and traditional practices for navigating and living in the Arctic environment.
communityEndorsement: Research conducted in partnership with Inuit community members using community-based participatory research methods. Community approval and involvement documented throughout research process.

title: Voices from the Bay: Traditional Ecological Knowledge of Inuit and Cree in the Hudson Bay Bioregion
description: Collaborative documentation of traditional ecological knowledge from Inuit and Cree communities around Hudson Bay, covering seasonal ice conditions, wildlife patterns, weather reading, and land-based skills.
communityEndorsement: Co-produced with Inuit and Cree community organizations. Community members are credited authors. Published with community consent and authorization through a formal collaborative research process.

title: The Walrus (Aiviq)
description: Inuit knowledge of the walrus (aiviq) including its cultural significance, traditional hunting practices, and role in Inuit material culture — from ivory carving to skin preparation. By an Inuit author, published by Inhabit Media.
communityEndorsement: Author Nadia Sammurtok is an Inuit woman from Rankin Inlet, Nunavut. Published by Inhabit Media, the Inuit-owned publisher in Iqaluit. Represents community-originated and community-controlled knowledge.

title: Inuit Qaujimajatuqangit: The Government of Nunavut's Approach to Policy Making
description: Official Government of Nunavut policy documentation integrating IQ (Inuit Qaujimajatuqangit) principles into governance and education. Describes the eight foundational IQ principles and their application to community programs.
communityEndorsement: Published by the Government of Nunavut, an Inuit-majority government. The Inuit Qaujimajatuqangit Katimajiit (IQK) provides oversight of IQ implementation. Official government policy documentation.

title: Siku: Knowing Our Ice — Documenting Inuit Sea Ice Knowledge and Use
description: Comprehensive documentation of Inuit knowledge about sea ice — terminology, navigation, hunting platforms, and seasonal change indicators. Bridges traditional knowledge with contemporary climate science.
communityEndorsement: Produced through community-based participatory research with Inuit hunters and elders from multiple communities. Community members reviewed and approved content. Inuit co-authors credited throughout.

title: Baidarka: The Kayak
description: Technical and historical documentation of the baidarka (Aleut/Inuit kayak), covering traditional construction methods, materials, and paddling techniques. Includes historical photographs and detailed construction drawings.
communityEndorsement: Author George Dyson conducted extensive research with Aleut communities and Alaska Native craftspeople. Note: As a non-Indigenous author, this work should be used alongside primary Inuit and Aleut sources. Most valuable for technical construction details.
